goal: Job queue: API handlers for job listing, cancellation, and monitoring

acceptance_criteria.description: GET /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/jobs returns paginated job list with status, type, created_at, started_at, completed_at, retry_count
GET /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/jobs/{jobId} returns full job detail including payload and last_error
POST /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/jobs/{jobId}/cancel transitions PENDING or RETRYING jobs to FAILED with cancellation reason. Returns 409 for RUNNING or already-terminal jobs.
POST /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/jobs/{jobId}/retry resets a FAILED job to PENDING with retry_count reset. Returns 409 for non-FAILED jobs.
GET /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/jobs/summary returns counts by status (pending, running, completed, failed) and avg execution time
All job endpoints require auth middleware. Cancel and retry require editor-or-above role.

critical: All endpoints scoped to engagement via URL param — no cross-engagement job access
Cancel and retry are write operations requiring editor+ role
Job list is tenant-scoped via RLS (no app-level filtering alone)
Cancel a RUNNING job is rejected (409) — only the worker can stop a running job
high: Pagination uses cursor-based approach (not OFFSET) for consistent results during inserts
Job summary endpoint is efficient (single GROUP BY query, not N+1)
WebSocket broadcasts job_cancelled event on successful cancel
Tests cover auth, pagination, cancel-running (409), retry-non-failed (409)
medium: Filter jobs by status and type via query params
Sort jobs by created_at or priority
